Representative_Docs: ['10 Scenic And Cultural Gems To Visit In Sri Lanka From ancient rock fortresses and sacred temples to misty tea plantations and tropical beaches, Sri Lanka offers a perfect blend of history, nature, and adventure. Explore 10 must-visit destinations including Sigiriya, Ella, Kandy, Mirissa, and Yala National Park for an unforgettable travel experience on this beautiful island nation. Sri Lanka may be a small island nation, but it is full of amazing nature, ancient history, stunning mountains and some of the most beautiful beaches on the globe. From misty tea estates and wildlife safaris to ancient temples and colonial towns, Sri Lanka is a beautiful mix of nature, culture and adventure. Whether you are a history buff, a beach bum or simply want a peaceful getaway, Sri Lanka has something to offer everyone.What’s unique about Sri Lanka is the diversity of its attractions that can be covered within a short travel distance. One moment you could be wandering around ancient ruins, the next, lounging on a tropical beach or seeing leopards in the wild. Here are 10 amazing places to visit in Sri Lanka that you definitely have to check out. Sigiriya is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in Sri Lanka that draws tourists from all over the world. The', 'Jamaica’s Tourism Industry now Rebounds with Sandals Dunn’s River Resort’s Grand Reopening Published on December 8, 2025 In a highly anticipated event for both locals and international travelers, Sandals Dunn’s River Resort in Ocho Rios, Jamaica, has officially reopened its doors, signaling a major milestone in the island’s tourism recovery efforts for 2025. After several weeks of preparation and tight collaboration between local communities, tourism partners, and the resort team, the revival of one of Jamaica’s most beloved all-inclusive resorts marks a turning point in the nation’s tourism industry, which was significantly impacted by the effects of Hurricane Melissa.The reopening of Sandals Dunn’s River Resort is not just a rejuvenation of a luxury hospitality icon, but also a key moment in Jamaica’s post-disaster tourism resurgence. Located along the beautiful North Coast of the island, the resort’s return marks the beginning of a broader tourism comeback that has been carefully planned for the past several months.Advertisement The Rebirth of Sandals Dunn’s River ResortSandals Dunn’s River Resort has long been one of Jamaica’s top all-inclusive destinations, offering travelers access to the pristine beaches of Ocho Rios, luxurious accommodations, and world-class amen', 'Villages in Northeast: Explore culture, nature, pottery and mask making ideal for slow travel New Delhi: December is one of the best months to explore the quieter corners of Northeast India, where winter brings clear skies, calm landscapes and a slower pace of life. Many villages across the region stand out for their scenic settings, cultural depth and welcoming communities. These destinations offer a mix of nature experiences, local traditions, wildlife trails, tribal heritage and winter-specific activities. From snow-covered valleys to riverside islands and clean eco-villages, each place tells its own story. Many of these locations also require permits, making planning an important part of the journey. For travellers looking to unwind, learn something new or simply escape crowded cities, these villages offer a refreshing end-of-year getaway. The list below highlights some of the most compelling options to explore in December. Late December turns Lachung and the nearby Yumthang Valley into a snow-filled landscape. The route offers views of frozen rivers and waterfalls, and travellers need a special permit for this region. Key experiences include seasonal flowers, hot springs, Shinba Rhododendron Sanctuary, Lachung Monastery, Zero Point and Mount Katao. The area is ideal fo']

Fraser Health said that it expects it to “go-live” later in the spring. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Health care workers pose in front of the da Vinci Xi System. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Surrey Memorial Hospital is one step closer to providing robotic-assisted surgery with the arrival of the da Vinci Xi Robotic Surgery system. Representative_Docs: ['Possibly injured bear roaming Frazer Lake area in Kelowna Published 1:35 pm Thursday, May 7, 2026 By Jordy Cunningham A possibly injured black bear is roaming the Upper Mission area, close to Frazer Lake in Kelowna. Residents are asked to avoid the area. (File photo) The BC Conservation Officer Service (BCCOS) is warning the public about a possibly injured black bear near a popular lake in Kelowna’s Upper Mission neighbourhood. On Wednesday night (May 6), BCCOS was called to the area for a report of an injured bear near Seon Crescent, close to Frazer Lake. However, officers were unable to locate the bear. While officers are continuing to search the area, they are asking the public to avoid walking in the Frazer Lake area. “Be aware of their surroundings and take precautions, including keeping pets at home and travelling in groups,” stated BCCOS to Black Press Media. Anyone who spots the bear should not approach it and is asked to report it to the RAPP line at 1-877-952-7277. Name: 53_traffic congestion_car park_traffic_birmingham
Representative_Docs: ['Croatia moves to tackle night-time disorder with local alcohol sales controls Whilst proposed amendments to the country’s Trade Act won’t include a nationwide ban on alcohol sales, cities and municipalities will be given the authority to regulate retail alcohol sales during evening and night-time hours, in line with local needs. Under the proposal, local authorities could restrict alcohol sales between 8pm and 6am, including in shops and online sales via delivery platforms, should they deem it necessary. Speaking on HRT’s main evening news Dnevnik, Tomislav Šuta, Mayor of Split, said the city council had already launched an initiative to amend the legal framework. He emphasised that the intention is not prohibition but addressing disorder in the historic city centre. “Split today is not the city I once recognised,” Šuta said, adding that authorities must listen to residents and ensure public order and peace. The city is also preparing a new communal order decision to enrich the old town’s offer while reducing inappropriate behaviour. Iva Rinčić, Mayor of Rijeka, agreed that new regulation may be necessary but cautioned against outright bans. She noted that while Rijeka does not face the same scale of challenges as Split, alcohol consumption among young peopl', 'Horns blaring in fury as shoppers become trapped in Trafford Centre car park \'hell\' Video footage showed horns blaring in fury as shoppers became trapped in car parks at Trafford Centre on Monday (December 29). Motorists were stuck in \'gridlocked\' queues at a number of car parks around the popular shopping centre throughout the afternoon and into the evening. Visitors headed to the huge shopping outlet for discounted sales following the Christmas period, but quickly took to social media after becoming trapped in their vehicles for a number of hours when trying to leave, with long delays and queues reported to the nearby M60 junction. Pictures shared on social media showed standstill traffic around the perimeter of the Trafford Centre, along Trafford Boulevard and Barton Dock Road, which was preventing shoppers from leaving various car parks. Some claimed they had been waiting for more than two hours. In one post on X, titled: "Hell at the Trafford Centre", long lines of cars can be seen waiting to exit the multi-storey car park. There is gridlocked queues with horns heard blaring in frustration from those attempting to leave. One shopper, Katrina Cliffe, was forced to abandon her car and help with traffic management on Monday evening, guiding other motorists away', 'UK city centre locked down with bars \'closed\' as police seal off area A UK city centre has been locked down with a driver arrested and a pedestrian rushed to hospital after an incident on Sunday evening. Bars and restaurants were closed off in Friar Lane in Nottingham after reports of a serious crash in the city centre. Floods of police and ambulances rushed into the area following the collision, with unnamed pedestrians being taken to the hospital with what are said to be serious injuries. The section of the city centre is a hotspot in the evenings; however, after the incident, it was left eerily quiet as many local businesses were forced to close early. Popular local spot Ned\'s Cavern posted on social media to its customers. Name: 54_santa_christmas_holiday season_festive
Representative_Docs: ['Santa Claus is coming to Grafton: VFW Post 3081 Auxiliary hosts Breakfast with Santa Families will be welcome to breakfast with Santa on Saturday, Dec. 6, from 9-11 a.m., at the VFW Post 3081 Post Home, located at 225 West Main St., Grafton. Reservations are not needed to take part in this cherished holiday tradition. GRAFTON, W.Va. — Christmas is fast approaching, and Santa Claus is taking a break from holiday preparations at the North Pole to visit Grafton for a special breakfast. The Veterans of Foreign Wars (VFW) Post 3081 Auxiliary has announced it will be hosting this festive event for the area’s children, giving them a chance to meet Santa. “This cherished holiday tradition is all about spreading Christmas joy and giving kids a chance to share their wishes with Santa,” said VFW member Amanda Bise. “Enjoy a hearty breakfast in a festive atmosphere while soaking in the spirit of the season.” Bise On Saturday, December 6, families are invited to the post home, located at 225 West Main Street in Grafton, for a delicious breakfast designed to create magical memories. The breakfast will be held from 9 to 11 a.m. The cost for the breakfast is just $5 per adult, and children will eat for free. “Bring your little ones to meet Santa Claus before he heads back to the N', 'Want an ‘ezbacke uvin’ or ‘mot cantrl’ for Christmas? Write to Santa The time has come again for both the young and old to send in their letters to Santa. This has become one of my favorite Christmas traditions. The letters to Santa we publish each December are often heartwarming and occasionally heartbreaking. They are a reminder of what makes Christmas magical. They are an amusing break from the sometimes jarring news we must print in the paper. And, hey, they might even be beneficial to your brain’s elasticity. It takes some creativity on the reader’s part to figure out just what the little kiddies are asking for, since we publish the letters verbatim. Try to decipher these phrases from previous Santa letters: I’m looking forward to the, umm, creative writing in this year’s batch of letters. We will publish Santa letters from children and adults in north central Idaho and southeastern Washington. The letters can be just a few words if you have a short wish list, but please try to write no more than 300 words. Be sure to include the author’s name, age and town of residence. The letters can be emailed to city@lmtribune.com, sent in the mail to Letters to Santa, Lewiston Tribune, 505 Capital St., Lewiston, ID 83501, or dropped off at our office. Name: 55_startups_dubai_companies_ces
Representative_Docs: ['INCHEON-IFEZ Booth Wraps Up a Successful CES 2026, Recording $560M in Export Consultations and 17 Innovation Awards CES 2026 INCHEON–IFEZ Booth Over 15,000 visitors to the INCHEON–IFEZ Booth; IFEZ supported a record 50 innovation companies participating in CES 2026. Participating companies secure a foundation for global expansion, including 20 MoU signings. INCHEON, South Korea, Jan. 16, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The Incheon Free Economic Zone (IFEZ) announced the successful operation of the INCHEON–IFEZ Booth at CES 2026, presenting IFEZ\'s future-city vision and the technologies of Incheon-based innovators to a global audience. Under the theme "Destination Incheon — From Smart City to AI City," the booth highlighted a sustainable urban future enabled by Incheon companies\' innovations and drew more than 15,000 visitors, according to IFEZ. During CES 2026, IFEZ also met with the Fairfax County Economic Development Authority (FCEDA) to discuss support measures and collaboration models for helping Incheon startups enter the U.S. market. The two sides plan to develop practical joint programs through continued cooperation. Name: 56_deer hunting_hunting_deer_state parks
Representative_Docs: ['Mid-season WMA waterfowl quota hunt app period to be held Oct. 22-Nov. 12 TWRA News Tennessee Wildlife Resources Agency (TWRA) announced that the application period for the 2025 Mid-Season Wildlife Management Area (WMA) Waterfowl Quota Hunt will be open from Wednesday, Oct. 22 through Wednesday, Nov. 12. Applications can be submitted online at gooutdoorstennessee.com, through the TWRA mobile app, at TWRA regional offices, or at any TWRA license agent. Mailed applications will not be accepted, and applicants must be at least 16 years old. Detailed application instructions, WMA locations, and general waterfowl quota hunt information are available on the TWRA Quota Hunts webpage. The deadline to submit applications is Wednesday, Nov. 12 at 11:59 p.m. (CST). A person may only submit one application, which can include up to 48 unique hunt choices; hunt choices may not be repeated. Qualifying parties must have at least four members, with a maximum of eight. Successful applicants will receive an email with instructions to log in and must claim their permit by Monday, Nov. 24 at 11:59 p.m. CST. Each party member must claim their own permit. Permits will be available to print after they have been claimed. Name: 57_retailers_supermarket_stores_restaurants
Representative_Docs: ["Major UK department store chain in working from home crackdown telling staff to be at office ‘more in person’ JOHN LEWIS has told staff it wants to see more of them back in the office to ensure it can push ahead with its recovery. The employee-owned business has reminded its workers — known as “partners” — that it expects them to be at work “more in person than not”. Please provide a valid email. Your info will be used in accordance with our Privacy Policy You'll now receive top stories, breaking news, and more, straight to your email. That means being in the office, out with suppliers or visiting customers, rather than working from home. Bosses say the move is designed to improve collaboration, speed up decisions and strengthen the firm’s culture as it continues its turnaround. John Lewis, which also owns supermarket Waitrose, insisted it was not ripping up hybrid working, stressing that it remained in place. In the note to staff, it said many businesses had stronger teamwork, a better culture and improved performance from a hybrid model with more face-to-face interactions. read more on john lewis RISKY BUSINESS UK furniture brand stocked in M&S & John Lewis axes 300 jobs after collapse SHOP TO IT John Lewis is making major change in stores and shoppers will be delighted The partnership i", 'Convenience Store Market 2031 Outlook – A USD 1001 Bn Opportunity as APAC Leads Growth with Hyper Convenience Store Expansion says a 2026 Mordor Intelligence Report Hyderabad, Jan. 09, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- According to Mordor Intelligence, the convenience store market valued at around USD 704 billion in 2025, is projected to grow from USD 747 billion in 2026 to USD 1001.6 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 6%. Growth is fueled by urban migration, rising demand for quick-shopping experiences, and the integration of digital ordering, loyalty programs, and payment systems. Retailers are expanding prepared-food offerings, adding EV charging stations, and using real-time analytics to speed up restocking, boosting basket value even as fuel sales fluctuate. Active private-equity deals and M&A continue due to steady cash flows and consistent customer traffic, while regulatory incentives for alternative-energy solutions offer new revenue opportunities. Moderate competition, with top chains holding a limited share, opens room for regional and mid-tier players to gain an edge through localized strategies, diversified products, and data-driven promotions. Regional Market Outlook Asia-Pacific Asia-Pacific is emerging as the fastest-growing region, driven by rising incomes and compact urban living.
